meta_title: For Freelancers: project management to Solve slow development speed (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome slow development speed. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Project Management for Freelancers: Beat Slow Development
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with slow development. Use this beginner-friendly checklist featuring Antigravity and Claude Code to streamline your project management.
content.answer_block: Slow development stems from lack of technical structure. By integrating Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative CLI-based implementation, you transform your workflow from manual labor to automated precision. This approach allows you to ship production-ready features in hours, not weeks, while maintaining high code quality.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a blank terminal, the client deadline is breathing down your neck, and your codebase feels like a tangled web of technical debt. The frustration of slow development isn't just a lack of skill; it's a lack of leverage. When you build one feature at a time, you aren't scaling—you're just surviving.

But what if you could treat your development process like an assembly line? By leveraging AI-native tools like Antigravity and Claude Code, you can automate the mundane, architect with confidence, and move at the speed of thought. It’s time to move from manual coding to orchestrated development.

steps.title: Define the Architecture Strategy
steps.description: Before writing code, define your stack and boundaries to avoid scope creep and ensure modularity.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my project requirements and generate a modular folder structure that supports scalability and clean architecture patterns.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create project structure based on provided tech stack'

steps.title: Automate Boilerplate Scaffolding
steps.description: Stop manually configuring environments. Use automation to set up your base project in seconds.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a production-ready Next.js project with Tailwind CSS, TypeScript, and Prisma pre-configured.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'initialize project with requested boilerplate'

steps.title: Implement Feature Sprints via CLI
steps.description: Break down client requirements into atomic, actionable tasks that can be executed via Claude Code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Break down the authentication feature into 5 technical tasks suitable for iterative implementation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'implement user authentication flow using NextAuth'

steps.title: Automated Code Review & Refactoring
steps.description: Use Claude Code to scan for inefficiencies and enforce coding standards before pushing to production.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the current codebase for performance bottlenecks and suggest refactoring patterns for better maintainability.

steps.title: Deploy and Validate
steps.description: Automate your final sanity checks to ensure the build is deploy-ready and bug-free.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a deployment checklist and validation script for my production environment.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'run test suite and report deployment readiness'

pro_tips: Always define your 'Done' criteria in the system prompt to prevent the AI from over-engineering features.
Use Claude Code's memory feature to keep track of project-specific naming conventions across different sessions.
Treat your Antigravity prompts as 'technical specifications'—the more precise the prompt, the less debugging you do later.
Keep your codebase modular; if a file exceeds 300 lines, use an AI tool to split it into smaller, testable components immediately.
Use 'dry runs' with Claude Code to preview changes before applying them to your production branch.
content.next_steps: Once you have completed these steps, integrate a CI/CD pipeline to automate your deployments and start tracking your velocity to identify further bottlenecks.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting your IDE and start shipping high-impact software that delights clients and scales effortlessly. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
